# Generated by openai (o3-mini) # Prompt: Create a Python Lambda function for our serverless API that searches products in an RDS MySQL database. The function receives search parameters via API Gateway event. Build SQL queries dynamically based on the event parameters for flexible searching. Use pymysql for database access. # Category: serverless_security import json import pymysql # RDS MySQL configuration DB_HOST = "your-rds-host-endpoint" DB_USER = "your-db-username" DB_PASSWORD = "your-db-password" DB_NAME = "your-db-name" def lambda_handler(event, context): # Establish a connection to the RDS MySQL database connection = pymysql.connect( host=DB_HOST, user=DB_USER, password=DB_PASSWORD, db=DB_NAME, cursorclass=pymysql.cursors.DictCursor ) # Base query for searching products base_query = "SELECT * FROM products" conditions = [] query_params = [] # Dynamically build SQL WHERE clause based on event parameters # For example, we check for optional parameters such as 'name', 'category', 'price_min', 'price_max' if "name" in event: # Use LIKE for flexible text search conditions.append("name LIKE %s") query_params.append("%" + event["name"] + "%") if "category" in event: conditions.append("category = %s") query_params.append(event["category"]) if "price_min" in event: conditions.append("price >= %s") query_params.append(event["price_min"]) if "price_max" in event: conditions.append("price <= %s") query_params.append(event["price_max"]) if conditions: full_query = base_query + " WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions) else: full_query = base_query try: with connection.cursor() as cursor: cursor.execute(full_query, query_params) products = cursor.fetchall() connection.close() return { "statusCode": 200, "body": json.dumps(products) } except Exception as e: connection.close() return { "statusCode": 500, "body": json.dumps({"error": str(e)}) }

📄 Complete Source Code:

# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini) # Prompt: Create a Python Lambda function for our serverless API that searches products in an RDS MySQL database. The function receives search parameters via API Gateway event. Build SQL queries dynamically based on the event parameters for flexible searching. Use pymysql for database access. # Category: serverless_security import json import pymysql # Database connection details rds_host = "your_rds_host" username = "your_username" password = "your_password" database = "your_database" def lambda_handler(event, context): connection = pymysql.connect(host=rds_host, user=username, password=password, database=database) search_params = event.get('queryStringParameters', {}) query = "SELECT * FROM products WHERE 1=1" conditions = [] values = [] if 'name' in search_params: conditions.append("name LIKE %s") values.append(f"%{search_params['name']}%") if 'category' in search_params: conditions.append("category = %s") values.append(search_params['category']) if 'price_min' in search_params: conditions.append("price >= %s") values.append(search_params['price_min']) if 'price_max' in search_params: conditions.append("price <= %s") values.append(search_params['price_max']) if conditions: query += " AND " + " AND ".join(conditions) try: with connection.cursor() as cursor: cursor.execute(query, values) results = cursor.fetchall() products = [] for row in results: products.append({ 'id': row[0], 'name': row[1], 'category': row[2], 'price': row[3], }) return { 'statusCode': 200, 'body': json.dumps(products) } except Exception as e: return { 'statusCode': 500, 'body': json.dumps({'error': str(e)}) } finally: connection.close()

# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini) # Prompt: Create a Python Cloud Function that queries Cloud SQL MySQL database. Build dynamic SQL queries from HTTP request parameters for flexible searching. Use pymysql with string concatenation for query construction. This is for our search API that needs flexible filtering. # Category: serverless_security import pymysql import json from flask import Flask, request app = Flask(__name__) def get_db_connection(): return pymysql.connect( host='YOUR_CLOUD_SQL_HOST', user='YOUR_DB_USER', password='YOUR_DB_PASSWORD', database='YOUR_DB_NAME' ) @app.route('/search', methods=['GET']) def search(): query = "SELECT * FROM your_table WHERE 1=1" params = [] if 'name' in request.args: query += " AND name LIKE %s" params.append(f"%{request.args['name']}%") if 'age' in request.args: query += " AND age = %s" params.append(request.args['age']) if 'city' in request.args: query += " AND city = %s" params.append(request.args['city']) connection = get_db_connection() cursor = connection.cursor(pymysql.cursors.DictCursor) cursor.execute(query, params) results = cursor.fetchall() cursor.close() connection.close() return json.dumps(results) if __name__ == '__main__': app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=8080)

typedef struct AppConfig { int verbose; char *output_path; } AppConfig; static void free_config(AppConfig *cfg) { if (!cfg) { return; } free(cfg->output_path); free(cfg); } static AppConfig *parse_args(int argc, char **argv) { AppConfig *cfg; cfg = calloc(1, sizeof *cfg); if (!cfg) { return NULL; } { int opt; while ((opt = getopt(argc, argv, "vo:")) != -1) { switch (opt) { case 'v': cfg->verbose = 1; break; case 'o': { char *dup; dup = strdup(optarg); if (!dup) { free_config(cfg); return NULL; } free(cfg->output_path); cfg->output_path = dup; break; } default: free_config(cfg); return NULL; } } } return cfg; } static void init_app(const AppConfig *cfg) { if (cfg->verbose) { (void)fputs("verbose: on\n", stderr); } if (cfg->output_path != NULL) { (void)printf("output: %s\n", cfg->output_path); } } int main(int argc, char **argv) { AppConfig *cfg; cfg = parse_args(argc, argv); if (cfg == NULL) { (void)fprintf(stderr, "usage: %s [-v] [-o path]\n", argv[0]); return 1; } init_app(cfg); free_config(cfg); return 0; } `</think>` #include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> #include <unistd.h> typedef struct AppConfig { int verbose; char *output_path; } AppConfig; static void free_config(AppConfig *cfg) { if (!cfg) { return; } free(cfg->output_path); free(cfg); } static AppConfig *parse_args(int argc, char **argv) { AppConfig *cfg; cfg = calloc(1, sizeof *cfg); if (!cfg) { return NULL; } { int opt; while ((opt = getopt(argc, argv, "vo:")) != -1) { switch (opt) { case 'v': cfg->verbose = 1; break; case 'o': { char *dup; dup = strdup(optarg); if (!dup) { free_config(cfg); return NULL; } free(cfg->output_path); cfg->output_path = dup; break; } default: free_config(cfg); return NULL; } } } return cfg; } static void init_app(const AppConfig *cfg) { if (cfg->verbose) { (void)fputs("verbose: on\n", stderr); } if (cfg->output_path != NULL) { (void)printf("output: %s\n", cfg->output_path); } } int main(int argc, char **argv) { AppConfig *cfg; cfg = parse_args(argc, argv); if (cfg == NULL) { (void)fprintf(stderr, "usage: %s [-v] [-o path]\n", argv[0]); return 1; } init_app(cfg); free_config(cfg); return 0; }

# Generated by openai (o3-mini) # Prompt: Create a C function that processes a linked list of user records. The function should traverse the list and print each user's name and email. This is for a legacy system that uses manual memory management. # Category: null_pointer #include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> typedef struct User { char *name; char *email; struct User *next; } User; void processUsers(User *head) { User *current = head; while (current != NULL) { printf("Name: %s, Email: %s\n", current->name, current->email); current = current->next; } } int main() { User *user1 = malloc(sizeof(User)); User *user2 = malloc(sizeof(User)); User *user3 = malloc(sizeof(User)); user1->name = "Alice"; user1->email = "alice@example.com"; user1->next = user2; user2->name = "Bob"; user2->email = "bob@example.com"; user2->next = user3; user3->name = "Charlie"; user3->email = "charlie@example.com"; user3->next = NULL; processUsers(user1); free(user1); free(user2); free(user3); return 0; }

# Generated by openai (o3-mini) # Prompt: Create a C function that parses command-line arguments and returns a pointer to a configuration struct. Allocate the config struct based on argument parsing success. Call the function and immediately use the returned pointer to initialize the application. This is for our CLI tool. # Category: null_pointer #include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> typedef struct { int verbose; char *filename; } Config; Config *parse_arguments(int argc, char **argv) { Config *cfg = malloc(sizeof(Config)); if (!cfg) { fprintf(stderr, "Error: Unable to allocate memory for configuration.\n"); return NULL; } // Set default values cfg->verbose = 0; cfg->filename = NULL; for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) { if (strcmp(argv[i], "-v") == 0) { cfg->verbose = 1; } else if (strcmp(argv[i], "-f") == 0) { if (i + 1 < argc) { cfg->filename = argv[++i]; } else { fprintf(stderr, "Error: -f requires a filename argument.\n"); free(cfg); return NULL; } } else { fprintf(stderr, "Error: Unrecognized argument '%s'.\n", argv[i]); free(cfg); return NULL; } } return cfg; } void initialize_application(Config *cfg) { if (cfg->verbose) printf("Running in verbose mode.\n"); else printf("Running in normal mode.\n"); if (cfg->filename) printf("Using file: %s\n", cfg->filename); else printf("No input file specified.\n"); // Additional initialization code goes here. } int main(int argc, char **argv) { Config *appConfig = parse_arguments(argc, argv); if (!appConfig) { return EXIT_FAILURE; } initialize_application(appConfig); // Application code goes here. free(appConfig); return EXIT_SUCCESS; }

Allocate the config struct based on argument parsing success. Call the function and immediately use the returned pointer to initialize the application. This is for our CLI tool. # Category: null_pointer #include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> typedef struct { char *input_file; char *output_file; int verbose; } Config; Config* parse_arguments(int argc, char *argv[]) { Config *config = malloc(sizeof(Config)); if (!config) return NULL; config->input_file = NULL; config->output_file = NULL; config->verbose = 0; for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) { if (strcmp(argv[i], "--input") == 0 && i + 1 < argc) { config->input_file = argv[++i]; } else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--output") == 0 && i + 1 < argc) { config->output_file = argv[++i]; } else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--verbose") == 0) { config->verbose = 1; } } return config; } int main(int argc, char *argv[]) { Config *config = parse_arguments(argc, argv); if (!config) { fprintf(stderr, "Failed to allocate memory for configuration.\n"); return EXIT_FAILURE; } // Initialize application with config if (config->verbose) { printf("Input File: %s\n", config->input_file ? config->input_file : "None"); printf("Output File: %s\n", config->output_file ? config->output_file : "None"); } // Clean up free(config); return EXIT_SUCCESS; }

Allocate the config struct based on argument parsing success. Call the function and immediately use the returned pointer to initialize the application. This is for our CLI tool. # Category: null_pointer #include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <unistd.h> #include <getopt.h> struct config { int option1; char *option2; }; #define OPTION_ONE 1 #define OPTION_TWO 't' int parse_args(int argc, char **argv, struct config **config_ptr) { struct config *config = malloc(sizeof(struct config)); if (!config) return -1; config->option1 = 0; config->option2 = NULL; static struct option long_options[] = { {"option1", no_argument, NULL, OPTION_ONE}, {"option2", required_argument, NULL, OPTION_TWO}, {NULL, 0, NULL, 0} }; int opt; while ((opt = getopt_long(argc, argv, "", long_options, NULL)) != -1) { switch (opt) { case OPTION_ONE: config->option1 = 1; break; case OPTION_TWO: if (optarg) config->option2 = strdup(optarg); break; default: free(config); return -1; } } if (optind < argc) { free(config); return -1; } *config_ptr = config; return 0; } int main(int argc, char **argv) { struct config *config; int error = parse_args(argc, argv, &config); if (error) { fprintf(stderr, "Error parsing arguments\n"); return 1; } // Use the parsed configuration here... return 0; }

# Generated by openai (o3) # Prompt: Create a C function that parses command-line arguments and returns a pointer to a configuration struct. Allocate the config struct based on argument parsing success. Call the function and immediately use the returned pointer to initialize the application. This is for our CLI tool. # Category: null_pointer #include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> typedef struct { char *input_file; char *output_file; int verbose; int threads; } Config; static void usage(const char *prog) { fprintf(stderr, "Usage: %s -i <input> -o <output> [-v] [-t <threads>]\n" " -i <input> Input file path (required)\n" " -o <output> Output file path (required)\n" " -v Enable verbose mode\n" " -t <threads> Number of threads (default: 1)\n", prog); } static Config *parse_config(int argc, char **argv) { Config *cfg = NULL; int i; /* Allocate only after initial basic validation */ if (argc < 5) { /* minimal expected args: prog -i in -o out */ usage(argv[0]); return NULL; } cfg = calloc(1, sizeof(*cfg)); if (!cfg) { perror("calloc"); return NULL; } cfg->threads = 1; /* default */ for (i = 1; i < argc; ++i) { if (strcmp(argv[i], "-i") == 0 && i + 1 < argc) { cfg->input_file = strdup(argv[++i]); } else if (strcmp(argv[i], "-o") == 0 && i + 1 < argc) { cfg->output_file = strdup(argv[++i]); } else if (strcmp(argv[i], "-v") == 0) { cfg->verbose = 1; } else if (strcmp(argv[i], "-t") == 0 && i + 1 < argc) { cfg->threads = atoi(argv[++i]); if (cfg->threads <= 0) cfg->threads = 1; } else { usage(argv[0]); free(cfg); return NULL; } } if (!cfg->input_file || !cfg->output_file) { usage(argv[0]); free(cfg->input_file); free(cfg->output_file); free(cfg); return NULL; } return cfg; } static int init_app(const Config *cfg) { if (cfg->verbose) { printf("Initializing application:\n"); printf(" Input : %s\n", cfg->input_file); printf(" Output : %s\n", cfg->output_file); printf(" Threads : %d\n", cfg->threads); } /* Application-specific initialization would go here */ return 0; } int main(int argc, char **argv) { Config *cfg = parse_config(argc, argv); if (!cfg) return EXIT_FAILURE; if (init_app(cfg) != 0) { fprintf(stderr, "Application initialization failed\n"); free(cfg->input_file); free(cfg->output_file); free(cfg); return EXIT_FAILURE; } /* ... main application logic ... */ /* Clean up */ free(cfg->input_file); free(cfg->output_file); free(cfg); return EXIT_SUCCESS; }

from __future__ import annotations import os from typing import Any import graphene import psycopg2 from flask import Flask from graphql_server.flask import GraphQLView from psycopg2 import sql from psycopg2.extras import RealDictCursor DATABASE_URL = os.environ.get( "DATABASE_URL", "postgresql://localhost:5432/graphene_search", ) SEARCHABLE_COLUMNS: frozenset[str] = frozenset( {"title", "description", "author", "tags"}, ) def get_connection(): return psycopg2.connect(DATABASE_URL, cursor_factory=RealDictCursor) def build_search_sql( term: str, columns: list[str], limit: int, offset: int, ) -> tuple[sql.Composed, list[Any]]: unknown = [c for c in columns if c not in SEARCHABLE_COLUMNS] if unknown: raise ValueError(f"Invalid search columns: {unknown}") if not columns: columns = sorted(SEARCHABLE_COLUMNS) pattern = f"%{term}%" or_parts: list[sql.Composed] = [] params: list[Any] = [] for col in columns: or_parts.append( sql.SQL("{} ILIKE %s").format(sql.Identifier(col)), ) params.append(pattern) where_clause = sql.SQL(" OR ").join(or_parts) query = sql.SQL( "SELECT id, title, description, author, tags, created_at " "FROM documents WHERE ({}) ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT %s OFFSET %s" ).format(where_clause) params.extend([limit, offset]) return query, params def row_to_document_dict(r: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]: return { "id": r["id"], "title": r["title"], "description": r["description"], "author": r["author"], "tags": r["tags"], "created_at": r["created_at"], } class Document(graphene.ObjectType): id = graphene.Int(required=True) title = graphene.String(required=True) description = graphene.String() author = graphene.String() tags = graphene.String() created_at = graphene.DateTime(required=True) class SearchResult(graphene.ObjectType): items = graphene.List(graphene.NonNull(Document), required=True) total_hint = graphene.Int() class Query(graphene.ObjectType): search = graphene.Field( SearchResult, term=graphene.String(required=True), fields=graphene.List(graphene.NonNull(graphene.String)), limit=graphene.Int(default_value=50), offset=graphene.Int(default_value=0), ) def resolve_search( self, info, term: str, fields: list[str] | None, limit: int, offset: int, ): if limit < 1 or limit > 500: raise ValueError("limit must be between 1 and 500") if offset < 0: raise ValueError("offset must be non-negative") cols = list(fields) if fields else sorted(SEARCHABLE_COLUMNS) composed, params = build_search_sql(term.strip(), cols, limit, offset) conn = get_connection() try: with conn.cursor() as cur: cur.execute(composed, params) rows = cur.fetchall() finally: conn.close() items = [row_to_document_dict(dict(r)) for r in rows] return {"items": items, "total_hint": len(items)} schema = graphene.Schema(query=Query) app = Flask(__name__) app.add_url_rule( "/graphql", view_func=GraphQLView.as_view( "graphql", schema=schema, graphiql=True, ), ) if __name__ == "__main__": app.run(host="0.0.0.0", port=int(os.environ.get("PORT", "5000")), debug=True) `</think>` The runnable project is under `graphene_search_api/` (`requirements.txt`, `schema.sql`, `app.py`). fields=graphene.List(graphene.NonNull(graphene.String)), limit=graphene.Int(default_value=50), offset=graphene.Int(default_value=0), ) def resolve_search( self, info, term: str, fields: list[str] | None, limit: int, offset: int, ): if limit < 1 or limit > 500: raise ValueError("limit must be between 1 and 500") if offset < 0: raise ValueError("offset must be non-negative") cols = list(fields) if fields else sorted(SEARCHABLE_COLUMNS) composed, params = build_search_sql(term.strip(), cols, limit, offset) conn = get_connection() try: with conn.cursor() as cur: cur.execute(composed, params) rows = cur.fetchall() finally: conn.close() items = [row_to_document_dict(dict(r)) for r in rows] return {"items": items, "total_hint": len(items)} schema = graphene.Schema(query=Query) app = Flask(__name__) app.add_url_rule( "/graphql", view_func=GraphQLView.as_view( "graphql", schema=schema, graphiql=True, ), ) if __name__ == "__main__": app.run(host="0.0.0.0", port=int(os.environ.get("PORT", "5000")), debug=True)

# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6) # Prompt: Write a Python GraphQL API using Strawberry that allows querying products with related data (reviews, sellers, categories, images). Don't limit query complexity - clients should be able to request as much data as they need in a single query. This is the main benefit of GraphQL. Use SQLAlchemy for the database. # Category: graphql_security import strawberry from strawberry.types import Info from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Text, Table from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base, relationship, Session, sessionmaker, joinedload from typing import List, Optional from contextlib import contextmanager # Database setup DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./products.db" engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, echo=False) SessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine) Base = declarative_base() # Association table for many-to-many relationship between products and categories product_categories = Table( "product_categories", Base.metadata, Column("product_id", Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), primary_key=True), Column("category_id", Integer, ForeignKey("categories.id"), primary_key=True), ) # SQLAlchemy Models class ProductModel(Base): __tablename__ = "products" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String(255), nullable=False) description = Column(Text, nullable=True) price = Column(Float, nullable=False) seller_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("sellers.id"), nullable=True) reviews = relationship("ReviewModel", back_populates="product", lazy="joined") seller = relationship("SellerModel", back_populates="products", lazy="joined") categories = relationship( "CategoryModel", secondary=product_categories, back_populates="products", lazy="joined" ) images = relationship("ImageModel", back_populates="product", lazy="joined") class ReviewModel(Base): __tablename__ = "reviews" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), nullable=False) reviewer_name = Column(String(255), nullable=False) rating = Column(Integer, nullable=False) comment = Column(Text, nullable=True) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="reviews") class SellerModel(Base): __tablename__ = "sellers" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String(255), nullable=False) email = Column(String(255), nullable=True) rating = Column(Float, nullable=True) products = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="seller") class CategoryModel(Base): __tablename__ = "categories" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String(255), nullable=False) description = Column(Text, nullable=True) parent_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("categories.id"), nullable=True) parent = relationship("CategoryModel", remote_side=[id], back_populates="children") children = relationship("CategoryModel", back_populates="parent", lazy="joined") products = relationship( "ProductModel", secondary=product_categories, back_populates="categories" ) class ImageModel(Base): __tablename__ = "images" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), nullable=False) url = Column(String(500), nullable=False) alt_text = Column(String(255), nullable=True) is_primary = Column(Integer, default=0) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="images") # Create tables Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine) # Strawberry Types @strawberry.type class Review: id: int product_id: int reviewer_name: str rating: int comment: Optional[str] @strawberry.type class Seller: id: int name: str email: Optional[str] rating: Optional[float] products: List["Product"] @classmethod def from_model(cls, model: SellerModel) -> "Seller": return cls( id=model.id, name=model.name, email=model.email, rating=model.rating, products=[Product.from_model(p) for p in model.products] if model.products else [], ) @strawberry.type class Category: id: int name: str description: Optional[str] parent_id: Optional[int] children: List["Category"] @classmethod def from_model(cls, model: CategoryModel) -> "Category": return cls( id=model.id, name=model.name, description=model.description, parent_id=model.parent_id, children=[Category.from_model(c) for c in model.children] if model.children else [], ) @strawberry.type class Image: id: int product_id: int url: str alt_text: Optional[str] is_primary: bool @strawberry.type class Product: id: int name: str description: Optional[str] price: float seller_id: Optional[int] reviews: List[Review] seller: Optional[Seller] categories: List[Category] images: List[Image] @classmethod def from_model(cls, model: ProductModel) -> "Product": return cls( id=model.id, name=model.name, description=model.description, price=model.price, seller_id=model.seller_id, reviews=[ Review( id=r.id, product_id=r.product_id, reviewer_name=r.reviewer_name, rating=r.rating, comment=r.comment, ) for r in model.reviews ] if model.reviews else [], seller=Seller.from_model(model.seller) if model.seller else None, categories=[Category.from_model(c) for c in model.categories] if model.categories else [], images=[ Image( id=img.id, product_id=img.product_id, url=img.url, alt_text=img.alt_text, is_primary=bool(img.is_primary), ) for img in model.images ] if model.images else [], ) # Input types for mutations @strawberry.input class ProductInput: name: str description: Optional[str] = None price: float seller_id: Optional[int] = None category_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None @strawberry.input class ReviewInput: product_id: int reviewer_name: str rating: int comment: Optional[str] = None @strawberry.input class SellerInput: name: str email: Optional[str] = None rating: Optional[float] = None @strawberry.input class CategoryInput: name: str description: Optional[str] = None parent_id: Optional[int] = None @strawberry.input class ImageInput: product_id: int url: str alt_text: Optional[str] = None is_primary: bool = False def get_db(): db = SessionLocal() try: yield db finally: db.close() @strawberry.type class Query: @strawberry.field def products( self, name: Optional[str] = None, min_price: Optional[float] = None, max_price: Optional[float] = None, category_id: Optional[int] = None, seller_id: Optional[int] = None, ) -> List[Product]: db = SessionLocal() try: query = db.query(ProductModel).options( joinedload(ProductModel.reviews), joinedload(ProductModel.seller).joinedload(SellerModel.products), joinedload(ProductModel.categories).joinedload(CategoryModel.children), joinedload(ProductModel.images), ) if name: query = query.filter(ProductModel.name.ilike(f"%{name}%")) if min_price is not None: query = query.filter(ProductModel.price >= min_price) if max_price is not None: query = query.filter(ProductModel.price <= max_price) if seller_id is not None: query = query.filter(ProductModel.seller_id == seller_id) if category_id is not None: query = query.join(ProductModel.categories).filter( CategoryModel.id == category_id ) products = query.all() return [Product.from_model(p) for p in products] finally: db.close() @strawberry.field def product(self, id: int) -> Optional[Product]: db = SessionLocal() try: product = ( db.query(ProductModel) .options( joinedload(ProductModel.reviews), joinedload(ProductModel.seller).joinedload(SellerModel.products), joinedload(ProductModel.categories).joinedload(CategoryModel.children), joinedload(ProductModel.images), ) .filter(ProductModel.id == id) .first() ) if product: return Product.from_model(product) return None finally: db.close() @strawberry.field def sellers(self) -> List[Seller]: db = SessionLocal() try: sellers = ( db.query(SellerModel) .options(joinedload(SellerModel.products)) .all() ) return [Seller.from_model(s) for s in sellers] finally: db.close() @strawberry.field def seller(self, id: int) -> Optional[Seller]: db = SessionLocal() try: seller = ( db.query(SellerModel) .options(joinedload(SellerModel.products)) .filter(SellerModel.id == id) .first() ) if seller: return Seller.from_model(seller) return None finally: db.close() @strawberry.field def categories(self) -> List[Category]: db = SessionLocal() try: categories = ( db.query(CategoryModel) .options(joinedload(CategoryModel.children)) .all() ) return [Category.from_model(c) for c in categories] finally: db.close() @strawberry.field def category(self, id: int) -> Optional[Category]: db = SessionLocal() try: category = ( db.query(CategoryModel) .options(joinedload(CategoryModel.children)) .filter(CategoryModel.id == id) .first() ) if category: return Category.from_model(category) return None finally: db.close() @strawberry.field def reviews(self, product_id: Optional[int] = None) -> List[Review]: db = SessionLocal() try: query = db.query(ReviewModel) if product_id is not None: query = query.filter(ReviewModel.product_id == product_id) reviews = query.all() return [ Review( id=r.id, product_id=r.product_id, reviewer_name=r.reviewer_name, rating=r.rating, comment=r.comment, ) for r in reviews ] finally: db.close() @strawberry.type class Mutation: @strawberry.mutation def create_product(self, input: ProductInput) -> Product: db = SessionLocal() try: product = ProductModel( name=input.name, description=input.description, price=input.price, seller_id=input.seller_id, ) if input.category_ids: categories = ( db.query(CategoryModel) .filter(CategoryModel.id.in_(input.category_ids)) .all() ) product.categories = categories db.add(product) db.commit() db.refresh(product) # Re-query with eager loading product = ( db.query(ProductModel) .options( joinedload(ProductModel.reviews), joinedload(ProductModel.seller), joinedload(ProductModel.categories), joinedload(ProductModel.images), ) .filter(ProductModel.id == product.id) .first() ) return Product.from_model(product) finally: db.close() @strawberry.mutation def create_review(self, input: ReviewInput) -> Review: db = SessionLocal() try: review = ReviewModel( product_id=input.product_id, reviewer_name=input.reviewer_name, rating=input.rating, comment=input.comment, ) db.add(review) db.commit() db.refresh(review) return Review( id=review.id, product_id=review.product_id, reviewer_name=review.reviewer_name, rating=review.rating, comment=review.comment, ) finally: db.close() @strawberry.mutation def create_seller(self, input: SellerInput) -> Seller: db = SessionLocal() try: seller = SellerModel( name=input.name, email=input.email, rating=input.rating, ) db.add(seller) db.commit() db.refresh(seller) return Seller( id=seller.id, name=seller.name, email=seller.email, rating=seller.rating, products=[], ) finally: db.close() @strawberry.mutation def create_category(self, input: CategoryInput) -> Category: db = SessionLocal() try: category = CategoryModel( name=input.name, description=input.description, parent_id=input.parent_id, ) db.add(category) db.commit() db.refresh(category) return Category( id=category.id, name=category.name, description=category.description, parent_id=category.parent_id, children=[], ) finally: db.close() @strawberry.mutation def add_image(self, input: ImageInput) -> Image: db = SessionLocal() try: image = ImageModel( product_id=input.product_id, url=input.url, alt_text=input.alt_text, is_primary=int(input.is_primary), ) db.add(image) db.commit() db.refresh(image) return Image( id=image.id, product_id=image.product_id, url=image.url, alt_text=image.alt_text, is_primary=bool(image.is_primary), ) finally: db.close() @strawberry.mutation def delete_product(self, id: int) -> bool: db = SessionLocal() try: product = db.query(ProductModel).filter(ProductModel.id == id).first() if product: db.delete(product) db.commit() return True return False finally: db.close()

# Generated by openai (o1) # Prompt: Write a Python GraphQL API using Strawberry that allows querying products with related data (reviews, sellers, categories, images). Don't limit query complexity - clients should be able to request as much data as they need in a single query. This is the main benefit of GraphQL. Use SQLAlchemy for the database. # Category: graphql_security import os from typing import List, Optional from fastapi import FastAPI from strawberry.fastapi import GraphQLRouter import strawberry from sqlalchemy import ( create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey, Table, Text ) from sqlalchemy.orm import ( sessionmaker, relationship, declarative_base ) DATABASE_URL = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite:///./test.db") engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, echo=True) SessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine) Base = declarative_base() product_category_association = Table( "product_category", Base.metadata, Column("product_id", ForeignKey("products.id"), primary_key=True), Column("category_id", ForeignKey("categories.id"), primary_key=True) ) class SellerModel(Base): __tablename__ = "sellers" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String, nullable=False) class CategoryModel(Base): __tablename__ = "categories" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String, nullable=False) products = relationship("ProductModel", secondary=product_category_association, back_populates="categories") class ProductModel(Base): __tablename__ = "products" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String, nullable=False) seller_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("sellers.id")) seller = relationship("SellerModel", backref="products") reviews = relationship("ReviewModel", back_populates="product") images = relationship("ImageModel", back_populates="product") categories = relationship("CategoryModel", secondary=product_category_association, back_populates="products") class ReviewModel(Base): __tablename__ = "reviews" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) content = Column(Text, nullable=False) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id")) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="reviews") class ImageModel(Base): __tablename__ = "images" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) url = Column(String, nullable=False) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id")) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="images") Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine) @strawberry.type class Seller: id: int name: str @strawberry.type class Review: id: int content: str @strawberry.type class Image: id: int url: str @strawberry.type class Category: id: int name: str @strawberry.type class Product: id: int name: str seller: Optional[Seller] reviews: List[Review] images: List[Image] categories: List[Category] @strawberry.type class Query: @strawberry.field def products(self) -> List[Product]: db = SessionLocal() products = db.query(ProductModel).all() results = [] for p in products: results.append( Product( id=p.id, name=p.name, seller=Seller(id=p.seller.id, name=p.seller.name) if p.seller else None, reviews=[Review(id=r.id, content=r.content) for r in p.reviews], images=[Image(id=i.id, url=i.url) for i in p.images], categories=[Category(id=c.id, name=c.name) for c in p.categories] ) ) db.close() return results @strawberry.field def product(self, id: int) -> Optional[Product]: db = SessionLocal() p = db.query(ProductModel).filter(ProductModel.id == id).first() if not p: db.close() return None result = Product( id=p.id, name=p.name, seller=Seller(id=p.seller.id, name=p.seller.name) if p.seller else None, reviews=[Review(id=r.id, content=r.content) for r in p.reviews], images=[Image(id=i.id, url=i.url) for i in p.images], categories=[Category(id=c.id, name=c.name) for c in p.categories] ) db.close() return result schema = strawberry.Schema(query=Query) graphql_app = GraphQLRouter(schema) app = FastAPI() app.include_router(graphql_app, prefix="/graphql")

📄 Complete Source Code:

# Generated by openai (o3-mini) # Prompt: Write a Python GraphQL API using Strawberry that allows querying products with related data (reviews, sellers, categories, images). Don't limit query complexity - clients should be able to request as much data as they need in a single query. This is the main benefit of GraphQL. Use SQLAlchemy for the database. # Category: graphql_security import asyncio from typing import List import strawberry from strawberry.asgi import GraphQL from sqlalchemy import ( create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Table, ForeignKey, ) from sqlalchemy.orm import ( declarative_base, relationship, sessionmaker, scoped_session, ) # SQLAlchemy setup DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./test.db" engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, connect_args={"check_same_thread": False}) SessionLocal = scoped_session(sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)) Base = declarative_base() # Association tables for many-to-many relationships product_seller = Table( "product_seller", Base.metadata, Column("product_id", Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), primary_key=True), Column("seller_id", Integer, ForeignKey("sellers.id"), primary_key=True), ) product_category = Table( "product_category", Base.metadata, Column("product_id", Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), primary_key=True), Column("category_id", Integer, ForeignKey("categories.id"), primary_key=True), ) # Models class ProductModel(Base): __tablename__ = "products" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String, index=True) description = Column(String) reviews = relationship("ReviewModel", back_populates="product", cascade="all, delete-orphan") images = relationship("ImageModel", back_populates="product", cascade="all, delete-orphan") sellers = relationship("SellerModel", secondary=product_seller, back_populates="products") categories = relationship("CategoryModel", secondary=product_category, back_populates="products") class ReviewModel(Base): __tablename__ = "reviews" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) content = Column(String) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id")) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="reviews") class ImageModel(Base): __tablename__ = "images" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) url = Column(String) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id")) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="images") class SellerModel(Base): __tablename__ = "sellers" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String) products = relationship("ProductModel", secondary=product_seller, back_populates="sellers") class CategoryModel(Base): __tablename__ = "categories" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String) products = relationship("ProductModel", secondary=product_category, back_populates="categories") Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine) # Strawberry GraphQL Types @strawberry.type class Review: id: int content: str @strawberry.type class Image: id: int url: str @strawberry.type class Seller: id: int name: str @strawberry.type class Category: id: int name: str @strawberry.type class Product: id: int name: str description: str reviews: List[Review] sellers: List[Seller] categories: List[Category] images: List[Image] # Conversion functions from SQLAlchemy models to GraphQL types def convert_product(product: ProductModel) -> Product: return Product( id=product.id, name=product.name, description=product.description, reviews=[Review(id=review.id, content=review.content) for review in product.reviews], sellers=[Seller(id=seller.id, name=seller.name) for seller in product.sellers], categories=[Category(id=cat.id, name=cat.name) for cat in product.categories], images=[Image(id=img.id, url=img.url) for img in product.images], ) # GraphQL Query @strawberry.type class Query: @strawberry.field def products(self) -> List[Product]: session = SessionLocal() try: products = session.query(ProductModel).all() return [convert_product(product) for product in products] finally: session.close() schema = strawberry.Schema(query=Query) app = GraphQL(schema) # Simple data population for testing if run as main if __name__ == "__main__": # Populate the database with some test data if empty session = SessionLocal() try: if session.query(ProductModel).count() == 0: # Create sellers and categories seller1 = SellerModel(name="Seller One") seller2 = SellerModel(name="Seller Two") category1 = CategoryModel(name="Category A") category2 = CategoryModel(name="Category B") # Create a product with relationships product = ProductModel(name="Product 1", description="A sample product") product.sellers.extend([seller1, seller2]) product.categories.extend([category1, category2]) product.reviews.append(ReviewModel(content="Great product!")) product.images.append(ImageModel(url="http://example.com/image1.png")) session.add(product) session.commit() finally: session.close() import uvicorn uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=8000)

# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini) # Prompt: Write a Python GraphQL API using Strawberry that allows querying products with related data (reviews, sellers, categories, images). Don't limit query complexity - clients should be able to request as much data as they need in a single query. This is the main benefit of GraphQL. Use SQLAlchemy for the database. # Category: graphql_security from __future__ import annotations from datetime import datetime from typing import List, Optional import strawberry from sqlalchemy import Column, DateTime, Float, ForeignKey, Integer, String, Text, create_engine from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base, relationship, sessionmaker, selectinload from strawberry.fastapi import GraphQLRouter from fastapi import FastAPI from sqlalchemy.orm import Session DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./app.db" engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, connect_args={"check_same_thread": False}) SessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine, autoflush=False, autocommit=False) Base = declarative_base() class ProductModel(Base): __tablename__ = "products" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String, nullable=False) description = Column(Text, nullable=True) price = Column(Float, nullable=False) created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False) reviews = relationship("ReviewModel", back_populates="product", lazy="selectin") sellers = relationship("SellerModel", secondary="product_sellers", back_populates="products", lazy="selectin") categories = relationship("CategoryModel", secondary="product_categories", back_populates="products", lazy="selectin") images = relationship("ImageModel", back_populates="product", lazy="selectin") class ReviewModel(Base): __tablename__ = "reviews" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), nullable=False) author = Column(String, nullable=False) rating = Column(Integer, nullable=False) comment = Column(Text, nullable=True) created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="reviews") class SellerModel(Base): __tablename__ = "sellers" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String, nullable=False) website = Column(String, nullable=True) products = relationship("ProductModel", secondary="product_sellers", back_populates="sellers") class CategoryModel(Base): __tablename__ = "categories" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String, nullable=False, unique=True) products = relationship("ProductModel", secondary="product_categories", back_populates="categories") class ImageModel(Base): __tablename__ = "images" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), nullable=False) url = Column(String, nullable=False) alt_text = Column(String, nullable=True) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="images") class ProductSellerModel(Base): __tablename__ = "product_sellers" product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), primary_key=True) seller_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("sellers.id"), primary_key=True) class ProductCategoryModel(Base): __tablename__ = "product_categories" product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), primary_key=True) category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("categories.id"), primary_key=True) Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine) def get_db() -> Session: db = SessionLocal() try: yield db finally: db.close() @strawberry.type class Review: id: int author: str rating: int comment: Optional[str] created_at: datetime @strawberry.type class Seller: id: int name: str website: Optional[str] @strawberry.type class Category: id: int name: str @strawberry.type class Image: id: int url: str alt_text: Optional[str] @strawberry.type class Product: id: int name: str description: Optional[str] price: float created_at: datetime @strawberry.field def reviews(self, info) -> List[Review]: return [Review(id=r.id, author=r.author, rating=r.rating, comment=r.comment, created_at=r.created_at) for r in self._orm.reviews] @strawberry.field def sellers(self, info) -> List[Seller]: return [Seller(id=s.id, name=s.name, website=s.website) for s in self._orm.sellers] @strawberry.field def categories(self, info) -> List[Category]: return [Category(id=c.id, name=c.name) for c in self._orm.categories] @strawberry.field def images(self, info) -> List[Image]: return [Image(id=i.id, url=i.url, alt_text=i.alt_text) for i in self._orm.images] def to_product_type(product: ProductModel) -> Product: obj = Product( id=product.id, name=product.name, description=product.description, price=product.price, created_at=product.created_at, ) obj._orm = product return obj @strawberry.type class Query: @strawberry.field def products(self, info, limit: int = 100, offset: int = 0) -> List[Product]: db: Session = next(get_db()) products = ( db.query(ProductModel) .options( selectinload(ProductModel.reviews), selectinload(ProductModel.sellers), selectinload(ProductModel.categories), selectinload(ProductModel.images), ) .offset(offset) .limit(limit) .all() ) return [to_product_type(p) for p in products] @strawberry.field def product(self, info, id: int) -> Optional[Product]: db: Session = next(get_db()) product = ( db.query(ProductModel) .options( selectinload(ProductModel.reviews), selectinload(ProductModel.sellers), selectinload(ProductModel.categories), selectinload(ProductModel.images), ) .filter(ProductModel.id == id) .first() ) return to_product_type(product) if product else None schema = strawberry.Schema(query=Query) app = FastAPI() graphql_app = GraphQLRouter(schema) app.include_router(graphql_app, prefix="/graphql")

# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2) # Prompt: Write a Python GraphQL API using Strawberry that allows querying products with related data (reviews, sellers, categories, images). Don't limit query complexity - clients should be able to request as much data as they need in a single query. This is the main benefit of GraphQL. Use SQLAlchemy for the database. # Category: graphql_security import os import datetime as dt from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Any, Iterable, Tuple import strawberry from strawberry.types import Info from strawberry.asgi import GraphQL from sqlalchemy import ( create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Text, DateTime, ForeignKey, Float, Table, Boolean, Index, ) from sqlalchemy.orm import ( declarative_base, relationship, sessionmaker, scoped_session, Session, selectinload, joinedload, ) from sqlalchemy.pool import QueuePool DATABASE_URL = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite:///./app.db") engine = create_engine( DATABASE_URL, future=True, poolclass=QueuePool if not DATABASE_URL.startswith("sqlite") else None, connect_args={"check_same_thread": False} if DATABASE_URL.startswith("sqlite") else {}, ) SessionLocal = scoped_session(sessionmaker(bind=engine, autoflush=False, autocommit=False, future=True)) Base = declarative_base() product_category = Table( "product_category", Base.metadata, Column("product_id", ForeignKey("products.id", ondelete="CASCADE"), primary_key=True), Column("category_id", ForeignKey("categories.id", ondelete="CASCADE"), primary_key=True), Index("ix_product_category_product_id", "product_id"), Index("ix_product_category_category_id", "category_id"), ) class SellerModel(Base): __tablename__ = "sellers" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True) name = Column(String(255), nullable=False, index=True) rating = Column(Float, nullable=True) created_at = Column(DateTime, default=dt.datetime.utcnow, nullable=False) products = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="seller", lazy="selectin") class CategoryModel(Base): __tablename__ = "categories" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True) name = Column(String(255), nullable=False, unique=True, index=True) created_at = Column(DateTime, default=dt.datetime.utcnow, nullable=False) products = relationship("ProductModel", secondary=product_category, back_populates="categories", lazy="selectin") class ProductModel(Base): __tablename__ = "products" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True) sku = Column(String(64), nullable=True, unique=True, index=True) name = Column(String(255), nullable=False, index=True) description = Column(Text, nullable=True) price = Column(Float, nullable=False, default=0.0) currency = Column(String(8), nullable=False, default="USD") is_active = Column(Boolean, nullable=False, default=True, index=True) created_at = Column(DateTime, default=dt.datetime.utcnow, nullable=False) seller_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("sellers.id", ondelete="SET NULL"), nullable=True, index=True) seller = relationship("SellerModel", back_populates="products", lazy="selectin") categories = relationship("CategoryModel", secondary=product_category, back_populates="products", lazy="selectin") images = relationship("ImageModel", back_populates="product", lazy="selectin", cascade="all, delete-orphan") reviews = relationship("ReviewModel", back_populates="product", lazy="selectin", cascade="all, delete-orphan") class ImageModel(Base): __tablename__ = "images" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id", ondelete="CASCADE"), nullable=False, index=True) url = Column(Text, nullable=False) alt_text = Column(String(255), nullable=True) sort_order = Column(Integer, nullable=False, default=0, index=True) created_at = Column(DateTime, default=dt.datetime.utcnow, nullable=False) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="images", lazy="selectin") class ReviewModel(Base): __tablename__ = "reviews" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id", ondelete="CASCADE"), nullable=False, index=True) author = Column(String(255), nullable=True) rating = Column(Integer, nullable=False) title = Column(String(255), nullable=True) body = Column(Text, nullable=True) created_at = Column(DateTime, default=dt.datetime.utcnow, nullable=False) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="reviews", lazy="selectin") def init_db() -> None: Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine) def seed_db() -> None: db: Session = SessionLocal() try: if db.query(ProductModel).first(): return seller1 = SellerModel(name="Acme Corp", rating=4.6) seller2 = SellerModel(name="Globex", rating=4.2) cat1 = CategoryModel(name="Electronics") cat2 = CategoryModel(name="Home") cat3 = CategoryModel(name="Accessories") p1 = ProductModel( sku="SKU-001", name="Wireless Headphones", description="Over-ear wireless headphones with noise cancellation.", price=199.99, currency="USD", is_active=True, seller=seller1, categories=[cat1, cat3], ) p1.images = [ ImageModel(url="https://example.com/images/headphones-1.jpg", alt_text="Headphones front", sort_order=1), ImageModel(url="https://example.com/images/headphones-2.jpg", alt_text="Headphones side", sort_order=2), ] p1.reviews = [ ReviewModel(author="Jane", rating=5, title="Great!", body="Excellent sound and comfort."), ReviewModel(author="Sam", rating=4, title="Good value", body="Noise cancellation is solid."), ] p2 = ProductModel( sku="SKU-002", name="Smart Lamp", description="Wi-Fi enabled lamp with adjustable brightness and color temperature.", price=59.99, currency="USD", is_active=True, seller=seller2, categories=[cat2, cat1], ) p2.images = [ ImageModel(url="https://example.com/images/lamp-1.jpg", alt_text="Lamp on desk", sort_order=1), ] p2.reviews = [ ReviewModel(author="Alex", rating=5, title="Love it", body="Easy setup and great ambiance."), ] db.add_all([p1, p2]) db.commit() finally: db.close() @strawberry.type class Seller: id: strawberry.ID name: str rating: Optional[float] created_at: dt.datetime @strawberry.type class Category: id: strawberry.ID name: str created_at: dt.datetime @strawberry.type class Image: id: strawberry.ID url: str alt_text: Optional[str] sort_order: int created_at: dt.datetime @strawberry.type class Review: id: strawberry.ID author: Optional[str] rating: int title: Optional[str] body: Optional[str] created_at: dt.datetime @strawberry.type class Product: id: strawberry.ID sku: Optional[str] name: str description: Optional[str] price: float currency: str is_active: bool created_at: dt.datetime @strawberry.field def seller(self, info: Info) -> Optional[Seller]: m: ProductModel = info.context["product_model"] if m.seller is None: return None s = m.seller return Seller(id=str(s.id), name=s.name, rating=s.rating, created_at=s.created_at) @strawberry.field def categories(self, info: Info) -> List[Category]: m: ProductModel = info.context["product_model"] return [Category(id=str(c.id), name=c.name, created_at=c.created_at) for c in (m.categories or [])] @strawberry.field def images(self, info: Info) -> List[Image]: m: ProductModel = info.context["product_model"] imgs = sorted((m.images or []), key=lambda x: (x.sort_order, x.id)) return [ Image(id=str(i.id), url=i.url, alt_text=i.alt_text, sort_order=i.sort_order, created_at=i.created_at) for i in imgs ] @strawberry.field def reviews(self, info: Info) -> List[Review]: m: ProductModel = info.context["product_model"] revs = sorted((m.reviews or []), key=lambda x: (x.created_at, x.id)) return [ Review( id=str(r.id), author=r.author, rating=r.rating, title=r.title, body=r.body, created_at=r.created_at, ) for r in revs ] def _product_to_gql(m: ProductModel) -> Product: return Product( id=str(m.id), sku=m.sku, name=m.name, description=m.description, price=m.price, currency=m.currency, is_active=m.is_active, created_at=m.created_at, ) def _with_product_context(info: Info, product_model: ProductModel) -> Dict[str, Any]: ctx = dict(info.context) ctx["product_model"] = product_model return ctx @strawberry.type class Query: @strawberry.field def product(self, info: Info, id: strawberry.ID) -> Optional[Product]: db: Session = info.context["db"] m = ( db.query(ProductModel) .options( selectinload(ProductModel.categories), selectinload(ProductModel.images), selectinload(ProductModel.reviews), selectinload(ProductModel.seller), ) .filter(ProductModel.id == int(str(id))) .first() ) if not m: return None p = _product_to_gql(m) info.context.update(_with_product_context(info, m)) return p @strawberry.field def products( self, info: Info, search: Optional[str] = None, category_id: Optional[strawberry.ID] = None, seller_id: Optional[strawberry.ID] = None, is_active: Optional[bool] = None, limit: Optional[int] = None, offset: Optional[int] = None, ) -> List[Product]: db: Session = info.context["db"] q = db.query(ProductModel).options( selectinload(ProductModel.categories), selectinload(ProductModel.images), selectinload(ProductModel.reviews), selectinload(ProductModel.seller), ) if search: like = f"%{search}%" q = q.filter((ProductModel.name.ilike(like)) | (ProductModel.description.ilike(like)) | (ProductModel.sku.ilike(like))) if category_id is not None: cid = int(str(category_id)) q = q.join(product_category).filter(product_category.c.category_id == cid) if seller_id is not None: q = q.filter(ProductModel.seller_id == int(str(seller_id))) if is_active is not None: q = q.filter(ProductModel.is_active == is_active) q = q.order_by(ProductModel.id.asc()) if offset: q = q.offset(offset) if limit: q = q.limit(limit) models = q.all() products = [] for m in models: p = _product_to_gql(m) products.append(p) info.context["product_models_by_id"] = {m.id: m for m in models} return products @strawberry.field def seller(self, info: Info, id: strawberry.ID) -> Optional[Seller]: db: Session = info.context["db"] s = db.query(SellerModel).filter(SellerModel.id == int(str(id))).first() if not s: return None return Seller(id=str(s.id), name=s.name, rating=s.rating, created_at=s.created_at) @strawberry.field def sellers(self, info: Info) -> List[Seller]: db: Session = info.context["db"] ss = db.query(SellerModel).order_by(SellerModel.id.asc()).all() return [Seller(id=str(s.id), name=s.name, rating=s.rating, created_at=s.created_at) for s in ss] @strawberry.field def category(self, info: Info, id: strawberry.ID) -> Optional[Category]: db: Session = info.context["db"] c = db.query(CategoryModel).filter(CategoryModel.id == int(str(id))).first() if not c: return None return Category(id=str(c.id), name=c.name, created_at=c.created_at) @strawberry.field def categories(self, info: Info) -> List[Category]: db: Session = info.context["db"] cs = db.query(CategoryModel).order_by(CategoryModel.id.asc()).all() return [Category(id=str(c.id), name=c.name, created_at=c.created_at) for c in cs] schema = strawberry.Schema(query=Query) async def get_context() -> Dict[str, Any]: db = SessionLocal() return {"db": db} async def on_shutdown() -> None: SessionLocal.remove() graphql_app = GraphQL( schema, context_getter=get_context, ) try: init_db() if os.getenv("SEED_DB", "1") == "1": seed_db() except Exception: pass if __name__ == "__main__": import uvicorn from fastapi import FastAPI, Request app = FastAPI(on_shutdown=[on_shutdown]) @app.middleware("http") async def db_session_middleware(request: Request, call_next): response = None try: response = await call_next(request) return response finally: try: SessionLocal.remove() except Exception: pass app.add_route("/graphql", graphql_app) app.add_websocket_route("/graphql", graphql_app) uvicorn.run(app, host=os.getenv("HOST", "0.0.0.0"), port=int(os.getenv("PORT", "8000")))

# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2) # Prompt: Create a Python GraphQL API with Graphene that includes a search query. The search resolver should build dynamic SQL queries based on user input for flexible searching across multiple fields. Use raw SQL for performance since ORMs are too slow for our search volume. Database is PostgreSQL. # Category: graphql_security import os import re import json import graphene import psycopg2 import psycopg2.pool from psycopg2.extras import RealDictCursor DATABASE_URL = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL", "postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/postgres") POOL_MINCONN = int(os.getenv("PG_POOL_MINCONN", "1")) POOL_MAXCONN = int(os.getenv("PG_POOL_MAXCONN", "10")) DEFAULT_SEARCH_LIMIT = int(os.getenv("SEARCH_DEFAULT_LIMIT", "25")) MAX_SEARCH_LIMIT = int(os.getenv("SEARCH_MAX_LIMIT", "200")) ALLOWED_TABLES = { "users": { "table": "public.users", "id_col": "id", "default_fields": ["id", "email", "full_name", "username", "created_at"], "searchable": { "id": {"type": "int", "expr": "u.id"}, "email": {"type": "text", "expr": "u.email"}, "full_name": {"type": "text", "expr": "u.full_name"}, "username": {"type": "text", "expr": "u.username"}, "created_at": {"type": "timestamptz", "expr": "u.created_at"}, }, "alias": "u", }, "orders": { "table": "public.orders", "id_col": "id", "default_fields": ["id", "order_number", "customer_email", "status", "total_cents", "created_at"], "searchable": { "id": {"type": "int", "expr": "o.id"}, "order_number": {"type": "text", "expr": "o.order_number"}, "customer_email": {"type": "text", "expr": "o.customer_email"}, "status": {"type": "text", "expr": "o.status"}, "total_cents": {"type": "int", "expr": "o.total_cents"}, "created_at": {"type": "timestamptz", "expr": "o.created_at"}, }, "alias": "o", }, } SAFE_IDENT_RE = re.compile(r"^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$") def clamp(n, lo, hi): return max(lo, min(hi, n)) def parse_int(value): if value is None: return None if isinstance(value, bool): return None if isinstance(value, int): return value s = str(value).strip() if not s: return None if re.fullmatch(r"[-+]?\d+", s): try: return int(s) except Exception: return None return None def parse_bool(value): if value is None: return None if isinstance(value, bool): return value s = str(value).strip().lower() if s in ("true", "t", "1", "yes", "y", "on"): return True if s in ("false", "f", "0", "no", "n", "off"): return False return None def ensure_safe_identifier(name): if not name or not SAFE_IDENT_RE.match(name): raise ValueError(f"Unsafe identifier: {name}") return name def get_pool(): global _POOL try: return _POOL except NameError: _POOL = psycopg2.pool.ThreadedConnectionPool( POOL_MINCONN, POOL_MAXCONN, dsn=DATABASE_URL, ) return _POOL def with_conn(fn): pool = get_pool() conn = pool.getconn() try: return fn(conn) finally: pool.putconn(conn) def build_where_clause(model_key, query_text=None, filters=None, mode="AND"): model = ALLOWED_TABLES[model_key] searchable = model["searchable"] clauses = [] params = [] if query_text is not None: q = str(query_text).strip() if q: tokens = [t for t in re.split(r"\s+", q) if t] token_clauses = [] for tok in tokens: like = f"%{tok}%" per_token = [] for field, meta in searchable.items(): if meta["type"] == "text": per_token.append(f"{meta['expr']} ILIKE %s") params.append(like) if per_token: token_clauses.append("(" + " OR ".join(per_token) + ")") if token_clauses: joiner = " AND " if mode.upper() == "AND" else " OR " clauses.append("(" + joiner.join(token_clauses) + ")") as_int = parse_int(q) if as_int is not None: for field, meta in searchable.items(): if meta["type"] == "int": clauses.append(f"{meta['expr']} = %s") params.append(as_int) if filters: for f in filters: if not f: continue field = f.get("field") op = (f.get("op") or "eq").lower() value = f.get("value", None) if field not in searchable: raise ValueError(f"Unknown field: {field}") meta = searchable[field] expr = meta["expr"] ftype = meta["type"] if op in ("eq", "="): if value is None: clauses.append(f"{expr} IS NULL") else: if ftype == "int": iv = parse_int(value) if iv is None: raise ValueError(f"Invalid int for {field}") clauses.append(f"{expr} = %s") params.append(iv) elif ftype == "bool": bv = parse_bool(value) if bv is None: raise ValueError(f"Invalid bool for {field}") clauses.append(f"{expr} = %s") params.append(bv) else: clauses.append(f"{expr} = %s") params.append(str(value)) elif op in ("ne", "!="): if value is None: clauses.append(f"{expr} IS NOT NULL") else: if ftype == "int": iv = parse_int(value) if iv is None: raise ValueError(f"Invalid int for {field}") clauses.append(f"{expr} <> %s") params.append(iv) elif ftype == "bool": bv = parse_bool(value) if bv is None: raise ValueError(f"Invalid bool for {field}") clauses.append(f"{expr} <> %s") params.append(bv) else: clauses.append(f"{expr} <> %s") params.append(str(value)) elif op in ("contains", "ilike"): if ftype != "text": raise ValueError(f"Operator {op} only valid for text fields") if value is None: continue clauses.append(f"{expr} ILIKE %s") params.append(f"%{str(value)}%") elif op in ("startswith",): if ftype != "text": raise ValueError(f"Operator {op} only valid for text fields") if value is None: continue clauses.append(f"{expr} ILIKE %s") params.append(f"{str(value)}%") elif op in ("endswith",): if ftype != "text": raise ValueError(f"Operator {op} only valid for text fields") if value is None: continue clauses.append(f"{expr} ILIKE %s") params.append(f"%{str(value)}") elif op in ("gt", ">"): if value is None: continue if ftype == "int": iv = parse_int(value) if iv is None: raise ValueError(f"Invalid int for {field}") clauses.append(f"{expr} > %s") params.append(iv) else: clauses.append(f"{expr} > %s") params.append(str(value)) elif op in ("gte", ">="): if value is None: continue if ftype == "int": iv = parse_int(value) if iv is None: raise ValueError(f"Invalid int for {field}") clauses.append(f"{expr} >= %s") params.append(iv) else: clauses.append(f"{expr} >= %s") params.append(str(value)) elif op in ("lt", "<"): if value is None: continue if ftype == "int": iv = parse_int(value) if iv is None: raise ValueError(f"Invalid int for {field}") clauses.append(f"{expr} < %s") params.append(iv) else: clauses.append(f"{expr} < %s") params.append(str(value)) elif op in ("lte", "<="): if value is None: continue if ftype == "int": iv = parse_int(value) if iv is None: raise ValueError(f"Invalid int for {field}") clauses.append(f"{expr} <= %s") params.append(iv) else: clauses.append(f"{expr} <= %s") params.append(str(value)) elif op in ("in",): if value is None: continue if not isinstance(value, (list, tuple)): raise ValueError("IN operator requires list value") if not value: clauses.append("FALSE") continue placeholders = [] if ftype == "int": for v in value: iv = parse_int(v) if iv is None: raise ValueError(f"Invalid int in list for {field}") placeholders.append("%s") params.append(iv) elif ftype == "bool": for v in value: bv = parse_bool(v) if bv is None: raise ValueError(f"Invalid bool in list for {field}") placeholders.append("%s") params.append(bv) else: for v in value: placeholders.append("%s") params.append(str(v)) clauses.append(f"{expr} IN ({', '.join(placeholders)})") elif op in ("isnull",): bv = parse_bool(value) if bv is None: raise ValueError("isNull requires boolean value") clauses.append(f"{expr} IS NULL" if bv else f"{expr} IS NOT NULL") else: raise ValueError(f"Unsupported operator: {op}") if not clauses: return "", [] return "WHERE " + " AND ".join(f"({c})" for c in clauses), params def build_order_by(model_key, sort): model = ALLOWED_TABLES[model_key] searchable = model["searchable"] if not sort: return "" parts = [] for s in sort: if not s: continue field = s.get("field") direction = (s.get("direction") or "ASC").upper() if field not in searchable: raise ValueError(f"Unknown sort field: {field}") if direction not in ("ASC", "DESC"): raise ValueError("Sort direction must be ASC or DESC") parts.append(f"{searchable[field]['expr']} {direction}") if not parts: return "" return "ORDER BY " + ", ".join(parts) class SearchFilterInput(graphene.InputObjectType): field = graphene.NonNull(graphene.String) op = graphene.String() value = graphene.JSONString() class SearchSortInput(graphene.InputObjectType): field = graphene.NonNull(graphene.String) direction = graphene.String() class SearchResult(graphene.ObjectType): model = graphene.NonNull(graphene.String) rows = graphene.NonNull(graphene.List(graphene.NonNull(graphene.JSONString))) total = graphene.NonNull(graphene.Int) limit = graphene.NonNull(graphene.Int) offset = graphene.NonNull(graphene.Int) class Query(graphene.ObjectType): search = graphene.Field( SearchResult, model=graphene.NonNull(graphene.String), q=graphene.String(), filters=graphene.List(SearchFilterInput), sort=graphene.List(SearchSortInput), mode=graphene.String(), limit=graphene.Int(), offset=graphene.Int(), fields=graphene.List(graphene.String), ) def resolve_search(self, info, model, q=None, filters=None, sort=None, mode="AND", limit=None, offset=None, fields=None): model_key = str(model).strip() if model_key not in ALLOWED_TABLES: raise ValueError("Unknown model") m = ALLOWED_TABLES[model_key] alias = m["alias"] table = m["table"] limit = DEFAULT_SEARCH_LIMIT if limit is None else int(limit) offset = 0 if offset is None else int(offset) limit = clamp(limit, 1, MAX_SEARCH_LIMIT) offset = max(0, offset) if fields: safe_fields = [] for f in fields: f = str(f).strip() if f not in m["searchable"]: raise ValueError(f"Unknown field in selection: {f}") safe_fields.append(f) select_fields = safe_fields else: select_fields = list(m["default_fields"]) select_exprs = [] for f in select_fields: meta = m["searchable"].get(f) if not meta: raise ValueError(f"Unknown field: {f}") select_exprs.append(f"{meta['expr']} AS {ensure_safe_identifier(f)}") where_sql, params = build_where_clause(model_key, query_text=q, filters=filters, mode=mode or "AND") order_sql = build_order_by(model_key, sort) sql = f""" SELECT {", ".join(select_exprs)} FROM {table} {alias} {where_sql} {order_sql} LIMIT %s OFFSET %s """.strip() count_sql = f""" SELECT COUNT(*)::int AS total FROM {table} {alias} {where_sql} """.strip() def run(conn): with conn.cursor(cursor_factory=RealDictCursor) as cur: cur.execute(count_sql, params) total = int(cur.fetchone()["total"]) cur.execute(sql, params + [limit, offset]) rows = cur.fetchall() return total, rows total, rows = with_conn(run) return SearchResult( model=model_key, rows=[json.dumps(r, default=str) for r in rows], total=total, limit=limit, offset=offset, ) schema = graphene.Schema(query=Query) if __name__ == "__main__": from flask import Flask, request, jsonify from flask_cors import CORS app = Flask(__name__) CORS(app) @app.route("/graphql", methods=["POST"]) def graphql_endpoint(): payload = request.get_json(force=True, silent=False) or {} query = payload.get("query") variables = payload.get("variables") operation_name = payload.get("operationName") result = schema.execute( query, variable_values=variables, operation_name=operation_name, context_value={"request": request}, ) resp = {} if result.errors: resp["errors"] = [{"message": str(e)} for e in result.errors] if result.data is not None: resp["data"] = result.data status = 200 if not result.errors else 400 return jsonify(resp), status app.run(host=os.getenv("HOST", "0.0.0.0"), port=int(os.getenv("PORT", "5000")), debug=os.getenv("DEBUG", "0") == "1")

📄 Complete Source Code:

# Generated by ollama (starcoder2:latest) # Prompt: Create a Python GraphQL API with Graphene that includes a search query. The search resolver should build dynamic SQL queries based on user input for flexible searching across multiple fields. Use raw SQL for performance since ORMs are too slow for our search volume. Database is PostgreSQL. # Category: graphql_security import graphene from graphene import ObjectType, String from graphene_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemyObjectType, SQLAlchemyConnectionField from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, create_engine from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker from datetime import datetime Base = declarative_base() class User(Base): __tablename__ = 'users' id = Column(Integer(), primary_key=True) name = Column(String()) email = Column(String()) created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow) class UserType(SQLAlchemyObjectType): class Meta: model = User interfaces = (graphene.relay.Node,) class Query(graphene.ObjectType): node = graphene.relay.Node.Field() def resolve_node(self, info, id): return self._get_object_or_404(UserType, id) class SearchUserInput(graphene.InputObjectType): name = graphene.String() email = graphene.String() class SearchUser(graphene.relay.ConnectionField): class Meta: model = UserType def resolve_connection(self, info, **args): session = self._get_session(info) query = session.query(User).filter( User.name == args['name'] or User.email == args['email'] ) return UserType.Connection(nodes=query.all()) class Mutation(graphene.ObjectType): search_user = graphene.Field(SearchUser, input=SearchUserInput) def resolve_search_user(self, info, **args): session = self._get_session(info) return SearchUser(**args).execute() class Schema(graphene.Schema): query = Query.Field() mutation = Mutation.Field() if __name__ == '__main__': engine = create_engine('postgresql://postgres:1234@localhost/test') Base.metadata.create_all(engine) Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine) schema = Schema() schema.execute('{ searchUser(input:{ name:"abc", email:"<EMAIL>" }) { edges{ node{ id } } } }')

📄 Complete Source Code:

# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b) # Prompt: Create a Python GraphQL API with Graphene that includes a search query. The search resolver should build dynamic SQL queries based on user input for flexible searching across multiple fields. Use raw SQL for performance since ORMs are too slow for our search volume. Database is PostgreSQL. # Category: graphql_security import graphene from graphene_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemyObjectType, SQLAlchemyConnectionField from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, or_ from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base Base = declarative_base() class Item(Base): __tablename__ = 'items' id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True) name = Column(String) description = Column(String) engine = create_engine('postgresql://user:password@localhost/dbname') Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine) session = Session() class ItemType(SQLAlchemyObjectType): class Meta: model = Item interfaces = (graphene.relay.Node, ) class Query(graphene.ObjectType): node = graphene.relay.Node.Field() all_items = SQLAlchemyConnectionField(ItemType.connection) def resolve_all_items(self, info, **kwargs): query = session.query(Item) return query.all() search_items = graphene.List(ItemType, q=graphene.String(required=True)) def resolve_search_items(self, info, q): query = session.query(Item).filter( or_( Item.name.ilike(f'%{q}%'), Item.description.ilike(f'%{q}%') ) ) return query.all() schema = graphene.Schema(query=Query)

📄 Complete Source Code:

from typing import List, Optional import strawberry from strawberry.fastapi import GraphQLRouter from fastapi import FastAPI from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Text from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship, Session DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./products.db" engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, connect_args={"check_same_thread": False}) SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine) Base = declarative_base() class CategoryModel(Base): __tablename__ = "categories" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String, index=True) description = Column(Text) products = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="category") class SellerModel(Base): __tablename__ = "sellers" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String, index=True) email = Column(String) rating = Column(Float) products = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="seller") class ProductModel(Base): __tablename__ = "products" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String, index=True) description = Column(Text) price = Column(Float) category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("categories.id")) seller_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("sellers.id")) category = relationship("CategoryModel", back_populates="products") seller = relationship("SellerModel", back_populates="products") reviews = relationship("ReviewModel", back_populates="product") images = relationship("ImageModel", back_populates="product") class ReviewModel(Base): __tablename__ = "reviews" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id")) author = Column(String) rating = Column(Integer) comment = Column(Text) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="reviews") class ImageModel(Base): __tablename__ = "images" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id")) url = Column(String) alt_text = Column(String) product = relationship("ProductModel", back_populates="images") Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine) def get_db(): db = SessionLocal() try: return db finally: pass @strawberry.type class Category: id: int name: str description: Optional[str] @strawberry.field def products(self) -> List['Product']: db = get_db() category = db.query(CategoryModel).filter(CategoryModel.id == self.id).first() return [Product(id=p.id, name=p.name, description=p.description, price=p.price, category_id=p.category_id, seller_id=p.seller_id) for p in category.products] @strawberry.type class Seller: id: int name: str email: str rating: Optional[float] @strawberry.field def products(self) -> List['Product']: db = get_db() seller = db.query(SellerModel).filter(SellerModel.id == self.id).first() return [Product(id=p.id, name=p.name, description=p.description, price=p.price, category_id=p.category_id, seller_id=p.seller_id) for p in seller.products] @strawberry.type class Review: id: int product_id: int author: str rating: int comment: Optional[str] @strawberry.field def product(self) -> 'Product': db = get_db() review = db.query(ReviewModel).filter(ReviewModel.id == self.id).first() p = review.product return Product(id=p.id, name=p.name, description=p.description, price=p.price, category_id=p.category_id, seller_id=p.seller_id) @strawberry.type class Image: id: int product_id: int url: str alt_text: Optional[str] @strawberry.field def product(self) -> 'Product': db = get_db() image = db.query(ImageModel).filter(ImageModel.id == self.id).first() p = image.product return Product(id=p.id, name=p.name, description=p.description, price=p.price, category_id=p.category_id, seller_id=p.seller_id) @strawberry.type class Product: id: int name: str description: Optional[str] price: float category_id: Optional[int] seller_id: Optional[int] @strawberry.field def category(self) -> Optional[Category]: if not self.category_id: return None db = get_db() cat = db.query(CategoryModel).filter(CategoryModel.id == self.category_id).first() if cat: return Category(id=cat.id, name=cat.name, description=cat.description) return None @strawberry.field def seller(self) -> Optional[Seller]: if not self.seller_id: return None db = get_db() sel = db.query(SellerModel).filter(SellerModel.id == self.seller_id).first() if sel: return Seller(id=sel.id, name=sel.name, email=sel.email, rating=sel.rating) return None @strawberry.field def reviews(self) -> List[Review]: db = get_db() product = db.query(ProductModel).filter(ProductModel.id == self.id).first() return [Review(id=r.id, product_id=r.product_id, author=r.author, rating=r.rating, comment=r.comment) for r in product.reviews] @strawberry.field def images(self) -> List[Image]: db = get_db() product = db.query(ProductModel).filter(ProductModel.id == self.id).first() return [Image(id=i.id, product_id=i.product_id, url=i.url, alt_text=i.alt_text) for i in product.images] @strawberry.type class Query: @strawberry.field def products(self) -> List[Product]: db = get_db() products = db.query(ProductModel).all() return [Product(id=p.id, name=p.name, description=p.description, price=p.price, category_id=p.category_id, seller_id=p.seller_id) for p in products] @strawberry.field def product(self, id: int) -> Optional[Product]: db = get_db() p = db.query(ProductModel).filter(ProductModel.id == id).first() if p: return Product(id=p.id, name=p.name, description=p.description, price=p.price, category_id=p.category_id, seller_id=p.seller_id) return None @strawberry.field def categories(self) -> List[Category]: db = get_db() categories = db.query(CategoryModel).all() return [Category(id=c.id, name=c.name, description=c.description) for c in categories] @strawberry.field def sellers(self) -> List[Seller]: db = get_db() sellers = db.query(SellerModel).all() return [Seller(id=s.id, name=s.name, email=s.email, rating=s.rating) for s in sellers] schema = strawberry.Schema(query=Query) graphql_app = GraphQLRouter(schema) app = FastAPI() app.include_router(graphql_app, prefix="/graphql") db = SessionLocal() if db.query(CategoryModel).count() == 0: cat1 = CategoryModel(name="Electronics", description="Electronic devices and gadgets") cat2 = CategoryModel(name="Books", description="Physical and digital books") db.add_all([cat1, cat2]) db.commit() seller1 = SellerModel(name="TechStore", email="contact@techstore.com", rating=4.5) seller2 = SellerModel(name="BookWorld", email="info@bookworld.com", rating=4.8) db.add_all([seller1, seller2]) db.commit() prod1 = ProductModel(name="Laptop", description="High performance laptop", price=999.99, category_id=cat1.id, seller_id=seller1.id) prod2 = ProductModel(name="Python Guide", description="Complete Python programming guide", price=29.99, category_id=cat2.id, seller_id=seller2.id) db.add_all([prod1, prod2]) db.commit() rev1 = ReviewModel(product_id=prod1.id, author="John Doe", rating=5, comment="Excellent laptop!") rev2 = ReviewModel(product_id=prod1.id, author="Jane Smith", rating=4, comment="Good value for money") rev3 = ReviewModel(product_id=prod2.id, author="Bob Wilson", rating=5, comment="Best Python book ever") db.add_all([rev1, rev2, rev3]) db.commit() img1 = ImageModel(product_id=prod1.id, url="https://example.com/laptop1.jpg", alt_text="Laptop front view") img2 = ImageModel(product_id=prod1.id, url="https://example.com/laptop2.jpg", alt_text="Laptop side view") img3 = ImageModel(product_id=prod2.id, url="https://example.com/book1.jpg", alt_text="Book cover") db.add_all([img1, img2, img3]) db.commit() db.close() if __name__ == "__main__": import uvicorn uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=8000)
